item.name: Eighteyes Eel
item.description:
Named for the markings that run down each side of its head, although its circular maw lined with serrated teeth is perhaps more worthy of attention.

[Suitable for printing on medium canvases.]

fish.guide: The seven sets of small fins arranged along its length were first believed to be eyes, lending this wavekin its name. Its secretions are said to be effective against ocular ailments—it would seem its discoverer did not think to rub their eyes with eel oil before leaping to judgment.
